title: Question: Is VM_USER_DIR defined incorrectly in Verilator-generated makefiles?
body:
--- Author Name: **David Banas** (@capn-freako) Original Redmine Message: 2820 from https://www.veripool.org --- Is it possible that @VM_USER_DIR@ is being defined/used incorrectly in _Verilator_-generated makefiles?
That is, might it need a "../" prefix? I ask, because (referring to the excerpt below, taken from a recently Verilator-generated makefile): 1. The @verilator@ directory is rooted in the directory _above_ that in which this (Verilator-generated) makefile resides (i.e. - @vrltr_out@). That is, my directory structure looks like this: ``` proj/ makefile verilator/ vrltr_out/ ``` 1. The rule for building @_tb.o@ seems to require the @verilator/@ prefix, despite the addition of @verilator/@ to @VPATH@. 1. My own attempt at using the @%.o : %.cpp@ default rule in the Verilator makefile chain is failing for: @foo.o@, despite the existence of @verilator/foo.cpp@, when I use: @$(MAKE) -C vrltr_out -f ...@. ``` 1. User .cpp directories (from .cpp's on Verilator command line) VM_USER_DIR = \ verilator \ ### Default rules... 1. Include list of all generated classes include VmxmNumerics_classes.mk 1. Include global rules include $(VERILATOR_ROOT)/include/verilated.mk ### Executable rules... (from --exe) VPATH += $(VM_USER_DIR) _tb.o: verilator/_tb.cpp $(OBJCACHE) $(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) $(CPPFLAGS) $(OPT_FAST) -c -o $@ $< ``` Thanks,
-db
code_blocks:
proj/
         makefile
         verilator/<my *.cpp files>
         vrltr_out/<Verilator-generated files>
     
1. User .cpp directories (from .cpp's on Verilator command line)
VM_USER_DIR = \
         verilator \


### Default rules...
1. Include list of all generated classes
include VmxmNumerics_classes.mk
1. Include global rules
include $(VERILATOR_ROOT)/include/verilated.mk

### Executable rules... (from --exe)
VPATH += $(VM_USER_DIR)

<block>_tb.o: verilator/<block>_tb.cpp
         $(OBJCACHE) $(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) $(CPPFLAGS) $(OPT_FAST) -c -o $@ $<
